lead_profile_text: STL Switch — Renovation Delay. Large property (3-bed + extension, farmhouse feel). Works away most of the time. No long-term tenant wanted. Needs STL income to cover £1,000-1,200/mo mortgage. Builder delays ongoing as of April 2026 — update: 'sounds mostly cosmetic'. Target guest type: contractor/worker stays rather than tourist short breaks.

top_objections.text: Builder delays — property not ready for launch
top_objections.zac_response: Follow-up planned end of April; Apr update shows mostly cosmetic issues remaining

top_objections.text: Mortgage £1,000-1,200/mo is high relative to ~£1,500/mo net — thin margin
top_objections.zac_response: Net income covers mortgage with surplus; worker demand provides stability

top_questions.text: Can STL income cover my mortgage?
top_questions.answer: Gross ~£28,578/yr, net ~£18,400/yr (~£1,533/mo). Covers mortgage with some surplus

top_questions.text: What type of guests would I get?
top_questions.answer: Worker-type demand: contractors, hospital/solar project workers — suited to longer stays rather than tourist short breaks

top_questions.text: Can I switch from council tax to business rates?
top_questions.answer: Yes — potential zero rates via business rates switch discussed

what_moved_them: Worker demand stability — contractor/project workers provide reliable bookings
Council tax to business rates switch (potential zero rates)
what_fell_flat: Thin margin between mortgage costs and projected STL net

conversion_gaps.gap: Renovation not yet complete — builder delays
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Re-engage May/June 2026 when cosmetic work is complete

conversion_gaps.gap: Margin concern — mortgage close to projected net income
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Discuss refinancing to IO BTL to reduce mortgage costs; model the revised margin
